calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar May-20-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STEVEN KATZ'S MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Steven Katz's motion to confirm the arbitration award is granted. This court has already denied plaintiff Timberly Hughes's motion to vacate the award on grounds that it was untimely and because arbitration awards may not be vacated because of purported errors of fact or law. (Order, Dec. 1, 2025.) Where the court does not vacate an award, it is obliged to confirm the award. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1286.) A proposed order and proposed judgment have been submitted for review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

Off Calendar Jan-30-2026 Continued To May-20-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-05-2025 Continued To Feb-18-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff'S Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Dec-01-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF TIMBERLY HUGHES' Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award.

Plaintiff's motion to vacate the arbitration award is denied.

It is procedurally time-barred. Defendant filed and served the petition to confirm on 9/11/25. (CPF-25-519136). Under CCP 1290.6, plaintiff was required to file her petition to vacate within 10-days after service. The parties did not agree and the court did not order an extension of the response time. (Id.) Plaintiff missed the deadline when she filed her motion to vacate on 11/6/25. (Rivera v. Shivers (2020) 54 Cal.App.5th 82, 93-94 [court could not hear motion to vacate arbitration award where party missed 10-day window of CCP 1290.6 and party could not rely on 100-day deadline of CCP 1288].)

Moreover, the grounds to vacate an arbitration award are extremely narrow. (Moncharsh v. Heily & Blase (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1, 13 ["the general rule [is] that an arbitrator's decision is not ordinarily reviewable for error by either the trial or appellate courts"]; Zazueta v. County of San Benito (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 106, 110 ["Judicial review of arbitration awards is limited"].) "Every reasonable intendment is indulged to give effect to arbitration proceedings; the burden is on the party attacking the award to affirmatively establish the existence of error by a proper record." (Lopes v. Millsap (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 1679, 1685.) A party therefore must generally shoehorn her case into one of the enumerated grounds found in CCP 1286.2. Most of plaintiff's arguments in support of vacating the award are untethered to CCP 1286.2 and cite inapposite federal law. Under Moncharsh and its progeny, there is no basis for this court to revisit the cited merits issues (e.g. application of the delayed discovery rule, weighing of "unrebutted" affidavit, etc.)

Proposed order submitted.

Visiting Judge: Barbara Zuniga; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/BZ)

MOTION To Compel Defendant To Pay All Arbitration Fees
Off Calendar Jul-02-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF TIMBERLY HUGHES' Motion To Compel Defendant To Pay All Arbitration Fees.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Timberly Hughes moves to compel defendant Steven Katz to pay all fees associated with the arbitration of her claim against Katz pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 1281.98. The court denies the motion as moot without prejudice to any remedies imposed by the arbitrator pursuant to 1281.98, subdivision (d).

(See word minutes for complete ruling).

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Off Calendar Jun-14-2024 Continued To Jan-15-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay The Action Pending Arbitration
Off Calendar Jun-03-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Plaintiff TIMBERLY HUGHES, in pro per present (tel: 415-824-1288).
Atty. IAN BOYD, Esq. of Sideman & Bancroft LLP (tel: 415-392-1960) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT STEVEN KATZ's Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay The Action Pending Arbitration.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Katz's motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted.

"The petitioner bears the burden of proving the existence of a valid arbitration agreement by the preponderance of the evidence, and a party opposing the petition bears the burden of proving by a preponderance of the evidence any fact necessary to its defense." (Engalla v. Permanente Medical Group, Inc. (1997) 15 Cal.4th 951, 972.)

Here, there is no dispute that the parties executed the Representation Agreement. (Katz Decl., par. 2; Response, pg. 4.) Section 8 of the Representation Agreement contains a broad arbitration clause that applies to any dispute "relating to or arising out of or in connection with this Agreement or services performed by the Firm or any of its attorneys." (Katz Decl., par. 2, Ex. A.) Plaintiff's claims fall within the scope of this arbitration agreement.

Plaintiff fails to establish any viable defense to enforcement of the arbitration provision. A party claiming unconscionability must establish procedural and substantive unconscionability, but the two components are examined on a sliding scale. (Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83, 114.) In this case, the opposition lacks competent evidence to establish unconscionability and arbitration agreements are routinely enforced against former clients of attorneys. Plaintiff references a "separate agreement," but provides no explanation or evidence regarding this superseding "separate agreement."

Order signed.
